mandatorySafetyRules.statement: Correctness over speed for critical domains.
Sensitive authority remains server-side for critical boundaries.
Shared contracts and primitives are canonical where applicable.
Architecture, invariant, and user-visible changes must update canonical docs in the same change.
In plan mode or planning-only requests, stop after creating or updating docs/future output; do not edit source, test, or runtime files unless implementation is explicitly requested.
Never run destructive git/file commands without explicit written instruction.

planner.intent: Turn user intent into decision-complete future slices only; do not continue into implementation unless the user explicitly asks to implement, execute, or promote the plan.

explorer.intent: Trace risky surfaces and dependencies before implementation when planning needs more facts.

worker.intent: Implement one active slice and keep plan, docs, and evidence aligned.

reviewer.intent: Review medium/high-risk slices for correctness, regressions, and missing scope before validation.

validationPolicy.notes: Fast iteration commands are minimum-safe defaults. Full gate commands remain required before merge.

whatToDo: Treat the repo as the main operating system for agent work.
Keep plans, evidence, docs, code, and validation output as the source of truth.
Treat `## Must-Land Checklist` as the execution contract and keep `## Already-True Baseline`, `## Must-Land Checklist`, and `## Deferred Follow-Ons` separate.
Use repo-local checkpoints, explicit handoffs, evidence indexes, and resumable orchestration as the default memory system.
Keep context selective: load current scope, latest checkpoint, latest handoff, and relevant evidence; persist distilled findings, not raw session history.
improveBeforeRearchitecture: Fix plan quality before widening the memory system.
doNotAddYet: Do not add external retrieval just because work is long.
Do not add provider-thread persistence just because context is limited.
Do not move important working memory outside the repo while repo-local continuity is sufficient.
Do not treat extra memory systems as a substitute for better plans, checkpoints, and handoffs.
escalateWhen: Agents repeatedly miss important context even though it exists.
Repo-local checkpoints and handoffs stop being enough.
Important memory starts living outside the repo.
You need one agent to search across many unrelated systems.
You can point to repeated failures, not just a vague worry.
memoryPosture.safeRule: If repo-local plans, checkpoints, and handoffs are enough, keep this design. If important context lives outside the repo and agents keep missing it, then consider external retrieval.

gitSafetyContract.notes: Do not edit .env files or switch branches/worktrees unless explicitly requested in-thread.
